---
title: "Lecture 26: Redirecting Requests & Request Dispatching"
subtitle: "{{< meta course.number >}} - {{< meta course.title >}}"
author: "{{< meta instructor.name >}}"
date: "March 10, 2026"
code-annotations: below
---

## {#title-slide data-menu-title="Title Slide" background="#053660"}

<div class="custom-title">
[Lecture 26]{.baby-blue-text}
</div>

<div class="custom-subtitle">
[Redirecting Requests & Request Dispatching]{.white-text}
</div>

<div class="custom-subtitle3">
Week 7 | Unit III: Servlets<br>
{{< meta course.number >}} - {{< meta course.title >}}<br>
{{< meta instructor.name >}}, {{< meta instructor.title >}}
</div>

---

## {data-menu-title="Learning Objectives"}

[Learning Objectives]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- Understand when to use redirect vs forward
- Explain browser behavior in each case
- Pass data correctly during navigation
- Avoid common mistakes in servlet navigation logic
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Simple Analogy"}

[Simple Real-Life Analogy]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
- **Redirect**: Teacher says, "Go to Room 205 for this work." You leave and go yourself.
- **Forward**: Teacher sends your file internally to another faculty room. You stay in the same process.

Same idea in web apps:

- Redirect = browser gets a new URL and sends a new request.
- Forward = server passes the same request internally.
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Redirect Basics"}

[What is Redirect?]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.java}
response.sendRedirect("login.jsp");
```

::: {.body-text-m}
- Browser receives HTTP status (usually `302`)
- Browser makes a **new request** to target URL
- URL in address bar **changes**
- Request attributes from old request are **not available**
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Forward Basics"}

[What is RequestDispatcher Forward?]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```{.java}
RequestDispatcher rd = request.getRequestDispatcher("result.jsp");
rd.forward(request, response);
```

::: {.body-text-m}
- Happens **inside server**
- Uses **same request and response** objects
- URL in address bar usually **does not change**
- Request attributes remain available
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Redirect vs Forward"}

[Redirect vs Forward Comparison]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

| Feature | Redirect | Forward |
|---|---|---|
| Requests | 2 requests | 1 request |
| URL change | Yes | No |
| Request data | Lost | Preserved |
| Use case | Post-success navigation, external pages | Internal JSP/servlet handoff |
| Performance | Slightly slower | Faster |

---

## {data-menu-title="When to Redirect"}

[When Redirect is Better]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
Use redirect when:

- You want user to see the new URL
- You need to move user to another module/app
- You are implementing PRG pattern (Post-Redirect-Get)
- You want page refresh to avoid form resubmission
:::

```{.java}
// After successful form save
response.sendRedirect("success.jsp");
```

---

## {data-menu-title="When to Forward"}

[When Forward is Better]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
Use forward when:

- You want to show JSP with server-side processed data
- You need request attributes in next resource
- You are moving from controller servlet to view JSP
:::

```{.java}
request.setAttribute("studentName", "Aditi");
request.getRequestDispatcher("dashboard.jsp").forward(request, response);
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Common Flow"}

[Common AKTU Exam Flow Pattern]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.java}
protected void doPost(HttpServletRequest request, HttpServletResponse response)
    throws ServletException, IOException {

  String email = request.getParameter("email");
  String password = request.getParameter("password");

  if ("student@gcet.ac.in".equals(email) && "1234".equals(password)) {
    request.getSession().setAttribute("userEmail", email);
    response.sendRedirect("home.jsp");
  } else {
    request.setAttribute("error", "Invalid credentials");
    request.getRequestDispatcher("login.jsp").forward(request, response);
  }
}
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Passing Data"}

[How to Pass Data Correctly]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
For **forward**:

- use `request.setAttribute(...)`
- read with `${attributeName}` in JSP

For **redirect**:

- use query string (`success.jsp?msg=ok`) for tiny data
- or use session for temporary data
:::

```{.java}
// Redirect with query parameter
response.sendRedirect("success.jsp?status=ok");
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Common Mistakes"}

[Common Mistakes (Very Important)]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- Calling `forward()` after response is already committed
- Expecting request attributes after redirect
- Writing output, then trying redirect/forward
- Using relative paths incorrectly
- Forgetting `return;` after redirect in complex methods
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Mini Practice"}

[Mini Practice Question]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
Scenario:

1. Student submits registration form.
2. If data valid, show confirmation page and new URL should appear.
3. If data invalid, show same form with error message.

Think:

- Which case uses redirect?
- Which case uses forward?
:::

::: {.fragment .body-text-m}
Expected approach:

- Valid: `sendRedirect("confirmation.jsp")`
- Invalid: `forward(request, response)` to form JSP with error attribute
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Summary"}

[Summary]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
You should now be confident about:

- redirect vs forward differences
- selecting correct navigation type in servlet controllers
- preserving/passing data without confusion
- avoiding frequent beginner mistakes asked in AKTU exams
:::
